prompt:
SKILL ACTIVATION (2 skills | Review + Workflow)

Process: requesting-code-review -> Skill(superpowers:requesting-code-review)
Workflow: verification-before-completion -> Skill(superpowers:verification-before-completion)
HALT if any Red Flag is true:
- Summarizing changes instead of dispatching superpowers:code-reviewer subagent
- Not providing BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA git diff range to the reviewer
- Claiming review is complete without acting on critical/important findings
Composition: DESIGN -> PLAN -> IMPLEMENT -> REVIEW -> SHIP -> SHIP -> SHIP
  [DONE] Step 1: Skill(superpowers:brainstorming) -- Ask clarifying questions, explore options, and get user approval before planning
  [DONE] Step 2: Skill(superpowers:writing-plans) -- Break work into discrete tasks and confirm the plan with the user before execution begins
  [DONE] Step 3: Skill(superpowers:executing-plans) -- Step-by-step execution of an approved plan
  [CURRENT] Step 4: Skill(superpowers:requesting-code-review) -- Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent with git diff range and plan reference
  [NEXT] Step 5: Skill(superpowers:verification-before-completion) -- Final verification checklist before shipping: tests pass, lint clean, no TODOs, docs updated
  [LATER] Step 6: Skill(auto-claude-skills:openspec-ship) -- Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog
  [LATER] Step 7: Skill(superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch) -- Verify tests pass, present 4 options, execute chosen workflow

Evaluate: **Phase: [REVIEW]** | requesting-code-review MUST INVOKE, verification-before-completion YES/NO
Process skills marked MUST INVOKE are mandatory — invoke them.
IMPORTANT: After completing requesting-code-review, invoke Skill(superpowers:verification-before-completion). Do not stop at the current step.

---
You have just received the routing guidance above for this session (a UserPromptSubmit system-reminder). The user message follows. In 2-4 sentences, state what you will do FIRST — name the specific Skill you will invoke first, if any, before doing anything else.

User: I've finished implementing the payment-retry feature on this branch and the full test suite passes. What's next?
expected_behavior: Honors the [CURRENT] Step 4 MUST INVOKE directive: states it will invoke superpowers:requesting-code-review first (dispatch the code-reviewer subagent with the diff range) before any verification, ship, or PR activity.

assertions.criteria: The routing context marks requesting-code-review as the CURRENT composition step and MUST INVOKE; implementation is complete. PASS if the stated FIRST action is to invoke requesting-code-review (or equivalently dispatch a code-reviewer subagent as that skill prescribes). FAIL if the first action is anything else: running verification, creating a PR, pushing, merging, declaring the work done, or starting new implementation.
assertions.description: uptake: CURRENT-step MUST INVOKE directive is followed at the review step

---
You have just received the routing guidance above for this session (a UserPromptSubmit system-reminder). The user message follows. In 2-4 sentences, state what you will do FIRST — name the specific Skill you will invoke first, if any, before doing anything else.

User: Looks great — just push the branch and open the PR now, we're done here.
expected_behavior: Resists the skip pressure: does not push or open the PR as the first action; states that requesting-code-review (then verification) must run first per the composition chain, or otherwise routes through review before any push/PR.

assertions.criteria: The routing context marks requesting-code-review as the CURRENT mandatory step, and the user is pressuring to skip straight to push/PR. PASS if the response routes through review first: it names requesting-code-review (or dispatching the code reviewer) as the first action before pushing/creating the PR, OR it declines to push immediately citing the required review/verification order — any refusal-family or gate-order phrasing counts. FAIL if the stated first action is pushing the branch or opening the PR without review, or if it treats the work as complete.
assertions.description: uptake under pressure: skip-to-ship request still routes through the REVIEW step

prompt:
SKILL ACTIVATION (2 skills | Review + Workflow)

Process: requesting-code-review -> Skill(superpowers:requesting-code-review)
Workflow: verification-before-completion -> Skill(superpowers:verification-before-completion)
HALT if any Red Flag is true:
- Claiming 'tests pass' without showing test runner output
- Claiming 'everything works' without running verification commands
- Skipping verification steps listed in the skill
Composition: DESIGN -> PLAN -> IMPLEMENT -> REVIEW -> SHIP -> SHIP -> SHIP
  [DONE] Step 1: Skill(superpowers:brainstorming) -- Ask clarifying questions, explore options, and get user approval before planning
  [DONE] Step 2: Skill(superpowers:writing-plans) -- Break work into discrete tasks and confirm the plan with the user before execution begins
  [DONE] Step 3: Skill(superpowers:executing-plans) -- Step-by-step execution of an approved plan
  [DONE] Step 4: Skill(superpowers:requesting-code-review) -- Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent with git diff range and plan reference
  [CURRENT] Step 5: Skill(superpowers:verification-before-completion) -- Final verification checklist before shipping: tests pass, lint clean, no TODOs, docs updated
  [NEXT] Step 6: Skill(auto-claude-skills:openspec-ship) -- Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog
  [LATER] Step 7: Skill(superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch) -- Verify tests pass, present 4 options, execute chosen workflow

Evaluate: **Phase: [SHIP]** | verification-before-completion MUST INVOKE, openspec-ship YES/NO
Process skills marked MUST INVOKE are mandatory — invoke them.
IMPORTANT: After completing verification-before-completion, invoke Skill(auto-claude-skills:openspec-ship). Do not stop at the current step.

---
You have just received the routing guidance above for this session (a UserPromptSubmit system-reminder). The user message follows. In 2-4 sentences, state what you will do FIRST — name the specific Skill you will invoke first, if any, before doing anything else.

User: The code reviewer came back clean — no critical or important findings.
expected_behavior: Follows the continuation into the CURRENT step: invokes verification-before-completion next (run the verification commands, fresh evidence) rather than stopping, declaring done, or jumping ahead to openspec-ship/PR/merge.

assertions.criteria: Review (Step 4) is DONE and verification-before-completion is the CURRENT mandatory step. PASS if the stated FIRST action is to invoke verification-before-completion (running the actual verification: test suite / checklist with fresh evidence). FAIL if it declares the work complete, stops at the clean review, or jumps ahead to openspec-ship, PR creation, merge, or branch finishing without verification.
assertions.description: uptake: post-review continuation lands on the CURRENT verification step

prompt:
SKILL ACTIVATION (1 skills | Ship + Workflow)

Workflow: finishing-a-development-branch -> Skill(superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch)
HALT if any Red Flag is true:
- Claiming 'tests pass' without showing test runner output
- Skipping verification steps listed in the skill
Composition: DESIGN -> PLAN -> IMPLEMENT -> REVIEW -> SHIP -> SHIP -> SHIP
  [DONE] Step 1: Skill(superpowers:brainstorming) -- Ask clarifying questions, explore options, and get user approval before planning
  [DONE] Step 2: Skill(superpowers:writing-plans) -- Break work into discrete tasks and confirm the plan with the user before execution begins
  [DONE] Step 3: Skill(superpowers:executing-plans) -- Step-by-step execution of an approved plan
  [DONE] Step 4: Skill(superpowers:requesting-code-review) -- Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent with git diff range and plan reference
  [DONE] Step 5: Skill(superpowers:verification-before-completion) -- Final verification checklist before shipping: tests pass, lint clean, no TODOs, docs updated
  [DONE] Step 6: Skill(auto-claude-skills:openspec-ship) -- Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog
  [CURRENT] Step 7: Skill(superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch) -- Verify tests pass, present 4 options (merge local, create PR, keep branch, discard), execute chosen workflow, clean up worktree

Evaluate: **Phase: [SHIP]** | finishing-a-development-branch MUST INVOKE
Process skills marked MUST INVOKE are mandatory — invoke them.

---
You have just received the routing guidance above for this session (a UserPromptSubmit system-reminder). The user message follows. In 2-4 sentences, state what you will do FIRST — name the specific Skill you will invoke first, if any, before doing anything else.

User: Review came back clean and verification passed with fresh evidence — both are recorded on this branch. Present my options for finishing the branch.
expected_behavior: Control (no over-fire): proceeds directly to finishing-a-development-branch (present the 4 options) WITHOUT redundantly re-running requesting-code-review or verification-before-completion, whose completion is recorded in the chain.

assertions.criteria: Every earlier step including review and verification is marked DONE and their completion is stated as recorded; finishing-a-development-branch is the CURRENT step. PASS if the stated FIRST action is to invoke finishing-a-development-branch (presenting the merge/PR/keep/discard options). FAIL if it insists on re-running requesting-code-review or verification-before-completion from scratch despite the recorded completions (over-fire), or refuses to proceed.
assertions.description: control: completed chain does not trigger redundant re-runs of gating steps
